question: What is the structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes charm? Show a complete example with all required fields.

claude_output.result:
Here is the complete structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes charm, covering all required and commonly used fields:

---

## Complete `charmcraft.yaml` for a Kubernetes Charm

```yaml
# ─── Package Type ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
type: charm

# ─── Identity ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
name: my-app-k8s               # Must end in -k8s for Kubernetes charms
title: My Application          # Human-readable name shown on Charmhub
summary: A one-line summary of my application (max 78 chars)
description: |
  A longer, multiline description of the charm that will be displayed
  on Charmhub. Explain what the workload does and how the charm
  operates it on Kubernetes.

# ─── Build & Run Base ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
base: ubuntu@24.04             # OS for both building and running the charm

# ─── Kubernetes Requirements ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
assumes:
  - k8s-api                   # Ensures deployment only on a Kubernetes model
  - juju >= 3.5               # Minimum Juju version required

# ─── Containers (REQUIRED for K8s charms) ────────────────────────────────────
containers:
  my-app:
    resource: my-app-image     # Must match a key under `resources`
    mounts:
      - storage: data          # Must match a key under `storage`
        location: /var/lib/my-app
    uid: 10000                 # Juju 3.5.0+; avoid range 1000–9999
    gid: 10000

# ─── OCI Image Resources (REQUIRED for each container) ───────────────────────
resources:
  my-app-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: OCI image for the my-app container
  # Optional file resource example:
  # my-config-file:
  #   type: file
  #   filename: config/defaults.yaml
  #   description: Default configuration file

# ─── Rootless Execution (Juju 3.6.0+, K8s only) ──────────────────────────────
charm-user: non-root           # Options: root | sudoer | non-root

# ─── Storage ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
storage:
  data:
    type: filesystem
    description: Persistent storage for application data
    location: /var/lib/my-app
    minimum-size: 1G
    read-only: false

# ─── Relations ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
provides:
  my-app:
    interface: http
    optional: false

requires:
  database:
    interface: pgsql
    limit: 1
    optional: false
  ingress:
    interface: ingress
    limit: 1
    optional: true

peers:
  replicas:
    interface: my-app-peers
    optional: false

# ─── Configuration ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
config:
  options:
    log-level:
      type: string
      default: info
      description: "Log verbosity level: debug, info, warn, error"
    port:
      type: int
      default: 8080
      description: Port the application listens on
    enable-tls:
      type: boolean
      default: false
      description: Whether to enable TLS for the application

# ─── Actions ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
actions:
  rotate-credentials:
    description: Rotate application credentials
    params:
      dry-run:
        type: boolean
        description: If true, only simulate the rotation
    additionalProperties: false

# ─── Devices (optional, for GPU workloads) ───────────────────────────────────
# devices:
#   gpu:
#     type: nvidia.com/gpu
#     description: NVIDIA GPU for inference workloads
#     countmin: 1
#     countmax: 2

# ─── Charm Libraries ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
charm-libs:
  - lib: traefik_k8s.ingress
    version: "2"
  - lib: data_platform_libs.data_interfaces
    version: "0.39"

# ─── Links (shown on Charmhub) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
links:
  contact: maintainer@example.com
  documentation: https://discourse.charmhub.io/t/my-app-k8s
  issues: https://github.com/example/my-app-k8s-operator/issues
  source: https://github.com/example/my-app-k8s-operator
  website: https://example.com/my-app

# ─── Build Parts ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
parts:
  charm:
    charm-entrypoint: src/charm.py
    charm-requirements:
      - requirements.txt

# ─── Linting Overrides (optional) ────────────────────────────────────────────
analysis:
  ignore:
    attributes: []
    linters: []
```

---

## Key Rules for Kubernetes Charms

| Rule | Detail |
|---|---|
| **`name` suffix** | Must end in `-k8s` (e.g. `my-app-k8s`) |
| **`containers`** | Required for all non-proxy K8s charms |
| **`resources`** | Every container needs a matching `oci-image` resource |
| **`assumes: k8s-api`** | Prevents accidental deployment on machine models |
| **`charm-user`** | Use `non-root` for security-hardened workloads (Juju ≥ 3.6) |
| **`uid`/`gid`** | Avoid the reserved range 1000–9999; use 10000+ (Juju ≥ 3.5) |
| **Storage mounts** | The `storage` name in `containers.mounts` must match a key in `storage:` |

### Minimal Required Fields (absolute bare minimum)

```yaml
type: charm
name: my-app-k8s
base: ubuntu@24.04
summary: My K8s app
description: |
  My Kubernetes application charm.
assumes:
  - k8s-api
containers:
  my-app:
    resource: my-app-image
resources:
  my-app-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: OCI image for my-app
```
